questions.text: Which of the following is a natural magnet?
questions.explanation: Lodestone (magnetite) is a naturally occurring magnetic mineral. It was discovered by ancient Greeks and used as the first compass.

questions.text: Magnetic strength is maximum at the middle of a bar magnet.
questions.explanation: Magnetic strength is maximum at the poles (ends) of a bar magnet and minimum at the center.

questions.text: What happens when a bar magnet is freely suspended?
questions.correctAnswer: It aligns itself in the north-south direction.
questions.explanation: A freely suspended bar magnet aligns with Earth's magnetic field, with its north pole pointing toward geographic north.

questions.text: The end of a magnet that points toward geographic north is called the ________ pole.
questions.explanation: The north-seeking pole (or north pole) of a magnet points toward Earth's geographic north when freely suspended.
